Up to {{jalink|1875|Q7764}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{jalink|1184|Q19728}} (Genryaku 1), {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445}} worshipped here while heading for {{jalink|Yashima|Q11465681}}{{jalink|2000|Q1985}}, {{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} * Associated deities - {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Suseribime|Q8191715|Suserihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Tamakushihime|Q11572681|Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{jalink|July 9|Q2690}} * Autumn grand festival - {{jalink|October 9|Q2923}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576}} on the {{jalink|Mugi Line|Q860478}} of {{jalink|JR Shikoku|Q496531|JR}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{jalink|Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632}} on the {{jalink|Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]]

Up to {{jalink|1875|Q7764}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{jalink|1184|Q19728}} (Genryaku 1), {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445}} worshipped here while heading for {{jalink|Yashima|Q11465681}}{{jalink|2000|Q1985}}, {{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} * Associated deities - {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Suseribime|Q8191715|Suserihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Tamakushihime|Q11572681|Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{jalink|July 9|Q2690}} * Autumn grand festival - {{jalink|October 9|Q2923}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576}} on the {{jalink|Mugi Line|Q860478}} of {{jalink|JR Shikoku|Q496531|JR}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{jalink|Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632}} on the {{jalink|Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]]

Up to {{jalink|1875|Q7764}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{jalink|1184|Q19728}} (Genryaku 1), {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445}} worshipped here while heading for {{jalink|Yashima|Q11465681}}{{jalink|2000|Q1985}}, {{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} * Associated deities - {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Suseribime|Q8191715|Suserihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Tamakushihime|Q11572681|Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{jalink|July 9|Q2690}} * Autumn grand festival - {{jalink|October 9|Q2923}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576}} on the {{jalink|Mugi Line|Q860478}} of {{jalink|JR Shikoku|Q496531|JR}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{jalink|Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632}} on the {{jalink|Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Up to {{jalink|1875|Q7764}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{jalink|1184|Q19728}} (Genryaku 1), {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445}} worshipped here while heading for {{jalink|Yashima|Q11465681}}{{jalink|2000|Q1985}}, {{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} * Associated deities - {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Suseribime|Q8191715|Suserihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Tamakushihime|Q11572681|Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{jalink|July 9|Q2690}} * Autumn grand festival - {{jalink|October 9|Q2923}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576}} on the {{jalink|Mugi Line|Q860478}} of {{jalink|JR Shikoku|Q496531|JR}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{jalink|Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632}} on the {{jalink|Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]]

Up to {{jalink|1875|Q7764}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{jalink|1184|Q19728}} (Genryaku 1), {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445}} worshipped here while heading for {{jalink|Yashima|Q11465681}}{{jalink|2000|Q1985}}, {{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} * Associated deities - {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Suseribime|Q8191715|Suserihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Tamakushihime|Q11572681|Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{jalink|July 9|Q2690}} * Autumn grand festival - {{jalink|October 9|Q2923}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576}} on the {{jalink|Mugi Line|Q860478}} of {{jalink|JR Shikoku|Q496531|JR}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{jalink|Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632}} on the {{jalink|Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Up to {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728}} (Genryaku 1), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445}} worshipped here while heading for {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681}}{{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985}}, {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} * Associated deities - {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{ill|lt=July 9|Q2690|WD=Q2690}} * Autumn grand festival - {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576|WD=Q6203576}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632|WD=Q11071632}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}{{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|WD=Q207309}}{{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|WD=Q11400186}} Nakayama 26 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格= former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Katsura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Katsura Shrine''' (かつらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|WD=Q11400186}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|WD=Q207309}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603). Up to {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728}} (Genryaku 1), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445}} worshipped here while heading for {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681}}{{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985}}, {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} * Associated deities - {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{ill|lt=July 9|Q2690|WD=Q2690}} * Autumn grand festival - {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576|WD=Q6203576}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632|WD=Q11071632}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Katsura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Katsura Shrine''' (かつらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|WD=Q11400186}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|WD=Q207309}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603). Up to {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728}} (Genryaku 1), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445}} worshipped here while heading for {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681}}{{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985}}, {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} * Associated deities - {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{ill|lt=July 9|Q2690|WD=Q2690}} * Autumn grand festival - {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576|WD=Q6203576}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632|WD=Q11071632}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Katsura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Katsura Shrine''' (かつらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|WD=Q11400186}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|WD=Q207309}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603). Up to {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728}} (Genryaku 1), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445}} worshipped here while heading for {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681}}{{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985}}, {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} * Associated deities - {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{ill|lt=July 9|Q2690|WD=Q2690}} * Autumn grand festival - {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576|WD=Q6203576}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632|WD=Q11071632}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Katsura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Katsura Shrine''' (かつらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|WD=Q11400186}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|WD=Q207309}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603). Up to {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728}} (Genryaku 1), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445}} worshipped here while heading for {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681}}{{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985}}, {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} * Associated deities - {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{ill|lt=July 9|Q2690|WD=Q2690}} * Autumn grand festival - {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576|WD=Q6203576}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632|WD=Q11071632}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Katsura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Katsura Shrine''' (かつらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|WD=Q11400186}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|WD=Q207309}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603). Up to {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728}} (Genryaku 1), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445}} worshipped here while heading for {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681}}{{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985}}, {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} * Associated deities - {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{ill|lt=July 9|Q2690|WD=Q2690}} * Autumn grand festival - {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576|WD=Q6203576}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632|WD=Q11071632}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Katsura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Katsura Shrine''' (かつらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|WD=Q11400186}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|WD=Q207309}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603). Up to {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728}} (Genryaku 1), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445}} worshipped here while heading for {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681}}{{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985}}, {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} * Associated deities - {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{ill|lt=July 9|Q2690|WD=Q2690}} * Autumn grand festival - {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576|WD=Q6203576}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632|WD=Q11071632}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Katsura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Katsura Shrine''' (かつらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|WD=Q11400186|1=Katsura chō}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|WD=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603). Up to {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} (Genryaku 1), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} worshipped here while heading for {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}}{{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}}, {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Associated deities - {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamakushi-hime}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{ill|lt=July 9|Q2690|WD=Q2690|1=July 9}} * Autumn grand festival - {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576|WD=Q6203576|1=Jizōbashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632|WD=Q11071632|1=Tokushima interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Katsura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Katsura Shrine''' (かつらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|WD=Q11400186|1=Katsura chō}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|WD=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603). Up to {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} (Genryaku 1), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} worshipped here while heading for {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}}{{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}}, {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Associated deities - {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamakushi-hime}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{ill|lt=July 9|Q2690|WD=Q2690|1=July 9}} * Autumn grand festival - {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576|WD=Q6203576|1=Jizōbashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632|WD=Q11071632|1=Tokushima interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Katsura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Katsura Shrine''' (かつらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|WD=Q11400186|1=Katsura chō}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|WD=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603). Up to {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} (Genryaku 1), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} worshipped here while heading for {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}}{{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}}, {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Associated deities - {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamakushi-hime}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{ill|lt=July 9|Q2690|WD=Q2690|1=July 9}} * Autumn grand festival - {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576|WD=Q6203576|1=Jizōbashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632|WD=Q11071632|1=Tokushima interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Katsura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Katsura Shrine''' (かつらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|WD=Q11400186|1=Katsura chō}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|WD=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603). Up to {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} (Genryaku 1), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} worshipped here while heading for {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}}{{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}}, {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Associated deities - {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamakushi-hime}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{ill|lt=July 9|Q2690|WD=Q2690|1=July 9}} * Autumn grand festival - {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576|WD=Q6203576|1=Jizōbashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632|WD=Q11071632|1=Tokushima interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Katsura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Katsura Shrine''' (かつらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|WD=Q11400186|1=Katsura chō}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|WD=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603). Up to {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} (Genryaku 1), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} worshipped here while heading for {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}}{{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}}, {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Associated deities - {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamakushi-hime}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{ill|lt=July 9|Q2690|WD=Q2690|1=July 9}} * Autumn grand festival - {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576|WD=Q6203576|1=Jizōbashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632|WD=Q11071632|1=Tokushima interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Katsura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Katsura Shrine''' (かつらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|WD=Q11400186|1=Katsura chō}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|WD=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603). Up to {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} (Genryaku 1), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} worshipped here while heading for {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}}{{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}}, {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Associated deities - {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamakushi-hime}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{ill|lt=July 9|Q2690|WD=Q2690|1=July 9}} * Autumn grand festival - {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576|WD=Q6203576|1=Jizōbashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632|WD=Q11071632|1=Tokushima interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima, Tokushima]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Katsura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Katsura Shrine''' (かつらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|WD=Q11400186|1=Katsura chō}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|WD=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603). Up to {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} (Genryaku 1), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} worshipped here while heading for {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}}{{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}}, {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Associated deities - {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamakushi-hime}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{ill|lt=July 9|Q2690|WD=Q2690|1=July 9}} * Autumn grand festival - {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576|WD=Q6203576|1=Jizōbashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632|WD=Q11071632|1=Tokushima interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima, Tokushima]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Katsura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Katsura Shrine''' (かつらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|WD=Q11400186|1=Katsura chō}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|WD=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603). Up to {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} (Genryaku 1), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} worshipped here while heading for {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}}{{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}}, {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Associated deities - {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamakushi-hime}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{ill|lt=July 9|Q2690|WD=Q2690|1=July 9}} * Autumn grand festival - {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576|WD=Q6203576|1=Jizōbashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632|WD=Q11071632|1=Tokushima interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima, Tokushima]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Katsura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Katsura Shrine''' (かつらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|WD=Q11400186|1=Katsura chō}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|WD=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603). Up to {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} (Genryaku 1), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} worshipped here while heading for {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}}{{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}}, {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Associated deities - {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamakushi-hime}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{ill|lt=July 9|Q2690|WD=Q2690|1=July 9}} * Autumn grand festival - {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576|WD=Q6203576|1=Jizōbashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632|WD=Q11071632|1=Tokushima interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima, Tokushima]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Katsura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Katsura Shrine''' (かつらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|WD=Q11400186|1=Katsura chō}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|WD=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603). Up to {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} (Genryaku 1), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} worshipped here while heading for {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}}{{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}}, {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Associated deities - {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamakushi-hime}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{ill|lt=July 9|Q2690|WD=Q2690|1=July 9}} * Autumn grand festival - {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576|WD=Q6203576|1=Jizōbashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632|WD=Q11071632|1=Tokushima interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima, Tokushima]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Katsura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Katsura Shrine''' (かつらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|WD=Q11400186|1=Katsura chō}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|WD=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603). Up to {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|WD=Q19728|1=1184}} (Genryaku 1), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} worshipped here while heading for {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}}{{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|WD=Q1985|1=2000}}, {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Associated deities - {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamakushi-hime}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{ill|lt=July 9|Q2690|WD=Q2690|1=July 9}} * Autumn grand festival - {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576|WD=Q6203576|1=Jizōbashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632|WD=Q11071632|1=Tokushima interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima, Tokushima]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|qid=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Katsura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Katsura Shrine''' (かつらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|qid=Q11400186|1=Katsura chō}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|qid=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|qid=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|qid=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|qid=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603). Up to {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|qid=Q7764|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|qid=Q19728|1=1184}} (Genryaku 1), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|qid=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} worshipped here while heading for {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|qid=Q11465681|1=Yashima}}{{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|qid=Q1985|1=2000}}, {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Associated deities - {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|qid=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|qid=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamakushi-hime}}, {{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{ill|lt=July 9|Q2690|qid=Q2690|1=July 9}} * Autumn grand festival - {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|qid=Q2923|1=October 9}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576|qid=Q6203576|1=Jizōbashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|qid=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|qid=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632|qid=Q11071632|1=Tokushima interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|qid=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima, Tokushima]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]]

Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|qid=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|qid=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}}{{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|qid=Q11400186|1=Katsura chō}} Nakayama 26 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格= former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|qid=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Katsura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Katsura Shrine''' (かつらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|qid=Q11400186|1=Katsura chō}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|qid=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|qid=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|qid=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|qid=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603). Up to {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|qid=Q7764|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|qid=Q19728|1=1184}} (Genryaku 1), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|qid=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} worshipped here while heading for {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|qid=Q11465681|1=Yashima}}{{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|qid=Q1985|1=2000}}, {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Associated deities - {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|qid=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|qid=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamakushi-hime}}, {{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{ill|lt=July 9|Q2690|qid=Q2690|1=July 9}} * Autumn grand festival - {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|qid=Q2923|1=October 9}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576|qid=Q6203576|1=Jizōbashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|qid=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|qid=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632|qid=Q11071632|1=Tokushima interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|qid=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima, Tokushima]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|qid=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Katsura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Katsura Shrine''' (かつらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Katsura-chō|Q11400186|qid=Q11400186|1=Katsura chō}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|qid=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|qid=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|qid=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|qid=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603). Up to {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|qid=Q7764|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), it was called Sugiō Daimyōjin, and there is a theory that {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} was its deity. It is said that on the lunar date of March 10 in {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|qid=Q19728|1=1184}} (Genryaku 1), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|qid=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} worshipped here while heading for {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|qid=Q11465681|1=Yashima}}{{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|qid=Q1985|1=2000}}, {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}, ''Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture''. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Associated deities - {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Suseribime|Q8191715|qid=Q8191715|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto|1=Suseri-Hime}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|lt=Tamakushihime|Q11572681|qid=Q11572681|lt=Tamakushihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamakushi-hime}}, {{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Sessha and Massha === * Konpira-gū, Fukeburi-gū, Akiba Shrine, Jisha Shrine === Festivals === * Summer grand festival - {{ill|lt=July 9|Q2690|qid=Q2690|1=July 9}} * Autumn grand festival - {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|qid=Q2923|1=October 9}} === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Jizōbashi Station|Q6203576|qid=Q6203576|1=Jizōbashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|qid=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|qid=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. * About 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tokushima Interchange|Q11071632|qid=Q11071632|1=Tokushima interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|qid=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === Notes === {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsura Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima, Tokushima]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]]
